item.title: The World Is Scrambling to Remake Semiconductor Supply Chains
item.description: On this episode of Bloomberg Primer, we explain how companies and governments are rethinking where chips are made.

item.title: White House Prepares Order to Boost AI Security, Hassett Says
item.description: The White House is looking into an executive order that would create a vetting system for new artificial intelligence models like Anthropic PBC’s Mythos in a bid to protect business and government networks from AI-related cyber risks, a top economic adviser said Wednesday.

item.title: Sweden’s Finance Ministry Sees Recovery Slowed by Iran War
item.description: Sweden’s finance ministry expects the Nordic country’s economic growth to be hit harder by the Middle East war than seen previously, potentially denting the government’s reelection prospects four months out from a parliamentary vote.

item.title: Viral $100 Kids Landline Startup Tin Can Debuts Bulk Order Program
item.description: Tin Can Untechnologies Inc., the startup behind the $100 landline for kids, is launching a bulk order program to make it easier for schools, sports teams and neighborhoods to get access to the viral product.

item.title: Google DeepMind Takes Minority Stake in Maker of ‘Eve Online’
item.description: Artificial intelligence research is plunging further into video games.

item.title: Poland Holds Rates as Iran Energy Shock Stokes Inflation
item.description: Poland’s central bank kept interest rates unchanged for a second month on Wednesday as the energy-price impact of the Iran war revives long-benign domestic inflation.

item.title: Time to Fight the Fed? Stocks Rally Faces Dashed Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: Is good news about the economy bad news for the stock market once again?

item.title: Greer Warns EU Tweaks Risk Watering Down US Trade Deal
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said changes the European Union is considering to a transatlantic trade deal could limit American exports to the bloc.

item.title: Shale Drillers Finally Heed Trump’s Call to Start Pumping More Oil
item.description: It’s taken a war in the Middle East and $100-a-barrel oil to convince companies.

item.title: Eli Lilly Kicks Off High-Grade Bond Sale in Push to Fund Deals
item.description: Eli Lilly & Co has kicked off the sale of investment-grade debt as part of an effort to fund its acquisition spree.

item.title: US Punts on Debt Auctions, Signaling No Changes Into 2027
item.description: The US Treasury signaled it’s still comfortable issuing the shortest-dated debt to meet escalating government borrowing needs — even as warnings emerge about the strategy’s risks.

item.title: Wall Street Readies Data Center IPOs as AI-Linked Debuts Surge
item.description: Wall Street banks are getting ready to raise billions of dollars taking data center companies public, even after IPO investors have already piled into anything that looks like a bet on artificial intelligence spending.

item.title: Swiss-EU Deal Needs Change of Constitution, Bern Lawmakers Say
item.description: A deal revamping Swiss relations with the European Union requires a constitutional amendment, according to a panel of lawmakers whose views hint at the hurdles the agreement will face.

item.title: US Firms Add 109,000 Jobs, Most Since Early 2025, ADP Says
item.description: US companies boosted payrolls in April by the most in over a year, the latest evidence of stabilization in the labor market.

item.title: AI Bots Auditioning for Wall Street Trading Are Mostly Losing
item.description: AI isn’t ready to replace your fund manager — and the public experiments testing it are showing why.

item.title: Nvidia Buys $500 Million of Rights for Stock in Corning
item.description: Nvidia Corp. has bought $500 million worth of rights for shares in the fiber-optic cable maker Corning Inc. as part of a broader partnership between the two companies aimed at expanding AI infrastructure.

item.title: Microsoft in Talks to Ax Energy Pledge Amid Data Center Boom
item.description: Microsoft Corp. may shelve one of the industry’s most ambitious clean-energy targets as it tries to remove hurdles that could hold it back in the race to power data centers, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 6, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.9% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after a report that the US believes it’s close to an agreement with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Singapore Defers Considering Political Pay Review on Iran War
item.description: Singapore will postpone considering an independent panel’s recommendations on the salaries of political office holders until the economic impact of the Iran war becomes clearer, a government minister said.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Debuts Crypto Trading, Undercuts Rivals on Price
item.description: Morgan Stanley is rolling out cryptocurrency trading on its E*Trade platform with a dig at rivals: cheaper pricing.

item.title: Emerging Stocks Hit Record High, Currencies Gain on Iran Signals
item.description: Emerging-market stocks rallied to a record high and currencies also jumped amid growing optimism that the US and Iran are moving closer to reaching a peace deal.

item.title: Nintendo Under Pressure to Raise Switch 2 Prices to Protect Margins
item.description: With shares on their worst streak in a decade, investors signal displeasure

item.title: Companies Rush to Sell Bonds in Europe’s Market at Record Pace
item.description: Companies are selling new debt in Europe at the busiest pace ever on Wednesday, storming into the market after earnings to lock in funding while borrowing costs remain low.

item.title: Mnuchin Says There Is No ‘Break-the-Glass’ Solution for US Borrowing
item.description: Treasury veterans warn of need to grapple with deepening deficits.

item.title: Egypt Inflation Unexpectedly Slows Despite Iran War Shocks
item.description: Egypt’s inflation rate unexpectedly slowed in April, even after the Iran war spurred higher fuel costs and a weakening of the currency.

item.title: Uber Beats on Bookings Outlook in Sign of Growing US Demand
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. provided a better-than-expected forecast for bookings, signaling that robust demand from US commuters and travelers will offset impact from geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Tech’s Red-Hot Rally Is Leaving Nvidia Far Behind
item.description: Nvidia’s grip on the AI processor market is seen as increasingly under threat

item.title: Disney’s Films, Parks Lift Profit in Debut Quarter For New CEO
item.description: Walt Disney Co. posted stronger results than Wall Street expected thanks to improved profitability at its streaming business, new Avatar and Zootopia movies, and guests spending more at the company’s resorts and on cruises.

item.title: Bank of Canada Weighs Clearer Playbook to Handle Supply Shocks
item.description: The Bank of Canada is weighing whether to spell out how it responds to supply shocks in its monetary policy framework, including how much emphasis it places on the strength of the economy when tackling inflation.

item.title: Ghana Inflation Snaps Lengthy Easing Streak on Iran War Impact
item.description: Ghana’s inflation rate increased for the first time in 16 months as the surge in global energy prices caused by the conflict in the Middle East drove up domestic fuel costs.
